tactical_shape: Enjoyed long spells of control but were worked hard by Austria's crisp passing and ferocious, scrappy press
Threat was minimal in the second half before late pressure and Messi's decisive finish
standout_players: Lionel Messi — record-breaking scorer, decisive late winner despite an early missed penalty
Emiliano Martínez — repelled a Marcel Sabitzer free-kick
Thiago Almada — intuitively let Facundo Medina's cutback run for the record goal
Leandro Paredes — found Messi for the winner after the loose ball
Julián Álvarez — had an effort saved in the build-up to the winner
home_read.momentum: Messi's relentless form and another landmark moment leave Argentina riding the emotional surge of a historic win.

tactical_shape: Crisp passing and a ferocious, scrappy press
Competent but blunt overall, and their tempo surprisingly slowed late on
standout_players: Alexander Schlager — beaten by Messi's record-breaking first-time finish
Marcel Sabitzer — forced Emiliano Martínez into a save from a free-kick
Patrick Wimmer — substitute who glanced a late chance wide
Xaver Schlager — brought into a challenge in the buildup to Argentina's opener
David Alaba — almost conceded an own goal after Messi's footwork
away_read.momentum: Austria were competitive and awkward for Argentina, but the late fade and inability to score leaves them with frustration rather than breakthrough momentum.
